mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 2% signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals a strengthening economy that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening economy periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 2% and a 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening supply chain and housing market that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 2% and HOUST rises > 1.2M in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply and housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% and a 3-month increase in payroll employment above 150,000 signals a tightening labor market and strong job growth that boosts wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls < 5% and PAYEMS rises > 150K in 3 months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor and strong job growth periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
